preferred_translation: when this is, this comes to be
definition: Core conditionality formula for the arising side of dependent arising.
notes: Use this record for the whole formula line rather than reconstructing it ad hoc from separate headwords. It governs the repository's default voice for the positive side of conditionality. The Buddhadasa usage profile supports treating this line as a practical and immediate formula rather than a scholastic abstraction.

context_rules.context: In standard conditionality and dependent-arising translation
context_rules.rendering: when this is, this comes to be

context_rules.context: When a more explicit present-tense phrasing is needed
context_rules.rendering: when this is present, this comes to be
context_rules.notes: Use this in explanatory prose only.

example_phrases.translation: when this is, this comes to be

authority_basis.scope: Supports treating the conditionality line as a practical and immediate formula rather than a scholastic abstraction.

translation_policy.default_scope: Use as the standard positive conditionality line in dependent-arising translation.
translation_policy.when_not_to_apply: Do not replace it with heavier causal wording unless a passage clearly demands explanatory expansion.
translation_policy.drift_risk: Prevents the core formula from drifting into generic cause-and-effect paraphrase.
